novel_features.name: Cross-source enrichment overlay
novel_features.description: Merge NutritionValue.org's derived analytics (net carbs and omega-6/omega-3 ratio) onto a USDA food record by its shared FDC id.
novel_features.rationale: NutritionValue.org's IDs are USDA FDC IDs, so the join is exact; NV computes net carbs and the omega-6/omega-3 ratio the USDA API never exposes, and this is the only tool that surfaces them programmatically.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Reach for this when a user wants net carbs or the omega-6/omega-3 ratio - numbers no USDA API call returns.

novel_features.name: Nutrient ranking
novel_features.description: Top or bottom foods by any single nutrient, filterable by category and dataset, ingested from NutritionValue.org's precomputed ranking pages.
novel_features.rationale: NutritionValue.org precomputes highest/lowest rankings for ~60 nutrients; no scriptable tool exposes them and the USDA API cannot rank across the database cheaply.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Reach for this to answer 'which foods are highest/lowest in nutrient X' without paging the whole database yourself.

novel_features.name: Common-basis comparison
novel_features.description: Compare 2-5 foods side by side on a common basis: per 100 g, per serving, or per 100 kcal (protein density).
novel_features.rationale: Requires a batch fetch plus local per-basis math; per-100kcal protein density is offered by no existing nutrition CLI.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Reach for this to rank foods by protein density or compare macros fairly across different serving sizes.

novel_features.name: Compound nutrient filters
novel_features.description: Find foods that satisfy multiple nutrient thresholds at once (e.g. protein >= 20 g AND kcal <= 165 per 100 g).
novel_features.rationale: The USDA API filters on one sort field; multi-condition thresholds require local filtering of nutrient arrays plus the synced store.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Reach for this for diet-constrained discovery like high-protein low-calorie foods in one query.

novel_features.name: Daily intake log with targets
novel_features.description: A persistent daily food diary with macro/micro targets, backed by local SQLite, with today/summary/progress reports.
novel_features.rationale: Stateful aggregation and target tracking live in local SQLite; the USDA API is stateless and has no logging surface.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Reach for this to record what a user ate and report progress against daily targets - a Claude-loggable tracker.

novel_features.name: Meal aggregation
novel_features.description: Total nutrition across several foods at given quantities in one stateless call (id:quantity pairs).
novel_features.rationale: Summing scaled nutrition across N foods requires batch fetch plus portion math; no USDA endpoint aggregates foods.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Reach for this to get the nutrition of a recipe or plate without recording it in the diary.

novel_features.name: Citation output
novel_features.description: Emit an APA or MLA citation for a USDA food record so agent output is verifiable and traceable to a real fdcId.
novel_features.rationale: Formats real USDA record metadata locally; provenance-grade citations are a requirement for agents and no CLI offers them.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Reach for this when a user needs a citable source for a nutrition figure in a report or agent answer.

narrative.headline: One agent-native CLI over USDA FoodData Central and NutritionValue.org - with cross-source enrichment, protein-density comparison, nutrient ranking, and a local SQLite cache no other nutrition tool ships.
narrative.value_prop: Look up any food's nutrition from USDA's ~600K-food database, then enrich it with NutritionValue.org's derived analytics (net carbs, omega-6/omega-3 ratio) that the USDA API never exposes. Compare foods on a per-100kcal protein-density basis, rank foods by any nutrient, filter by compound thresholds, and log a daily diary against targets - all offline-cached, cited, and agent-native.
narrative.auth_narrative: USDA FoodData Central needs a free api.data.gov key. Set FDC_API_KEY (or USDA_API_KEY). Without a key the CLI falls back to USDA's public DEMO_KEY (rate-limited to ~30 requests/hour). NutritionValue.org commands need no key.

quickstart.comment: Check API key config and reachability before anything else.
Find a food and grab a match's fdcId.
Full USDA nutrition record for cheddar (fdcId 173414).
Add NutritionValue.org derived analytics (net carbs, omega ratio).
Rank foods by protein density (grams protein per 100 kcal).

troubleshoots.symptom: HTTP 200 but the body is HTML, or 429 Too Many Requests
troubleshoots.fix: You are rate-limited. Set a real key: export FDC_API_KEY=<your-key> (DEMO_KEY allows only ~30/hour).

troubleshoots.symptom: food get returns sparse nutrients for a branded product
troubleshoots.fix: Branded foods carry label nutrients only; use --data-type Foundation or SR Legacy for a full nutrient profile.
NutritionValue.org data pages need a browser-like request; the CLI handles this, but retry once if the site is briefly slow, and keep requests polite.

troubleshoots.symptom: search returns branded junk instead of the generic food
troubleshoots.fix: Filter with --data-type Foundation,SR%20Legacy to prefer whole-food records.

narrative.when_to_use: Use this CLI when an agent or user needs trustworthy, citable food nutrition data: looking up macros and micros for a food, comparing foods by protein density or on a common basis, ranking foods by a nutrient, finding foods that meet compound dietary thresholds, enriching a USDA record with NutritionValue.org's derived analytics, or logging a daily food diary against targets. It is offline-cached and agent-native, so repeated lookups and aggregations are cheap and the numbers always trace to a real USDA fdcId.
anti_triggers: Do not use this CLI for restaurant chain menu items - USDA and NutritionValue.org do not carry curated restaurant menus (use Nutritionix or CalorieKing).
Do not use this CLI for glycemic index - neither source provides it.
Do not use this CLI to place grocery orders or track weight/biometrics - it is a nutrition-facts and diary tool, not a fitness app.
Do not use this CLI as a medical or clinical nutrition authority - it reports database values, not personalized dietary advice.

recipes.title: Protein density leaderboard
recipes.explanation: Compare foods by grams of protein per 100 kcal and pull only the name and density fields.

recipes.title: Low-carb shopping list
recipes.explanation: Get the 15 lowest-carbohydrate foods from NutritionValue.org's ranking pages.

recipes.title: Enrich a food with derived analytics
recipes.explanation: Merge NutritionValue.org's net carbs and omega-6/omega-3 ratio onto a USDA record.

recipes.title: Total a meal
recipes.explanation: Sum nutrition across cheddar and a banana at given gram quantities in one call.

recipes.title: Daily progress against targets
recipes.explanation: Report today's logged intake versus configured macro targets.
